english translation text:
In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

He frowned and turned away
‘Abasa – He Frowned

when the blind man approached him.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

And how do you know, maybe he would purify himself,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

or take admonition, and the admonition would benefit him!
‘Abasa – He Frowned

But as for someone who is wealthy,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

you attend to him,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

though you are not liable if he does not purify himself.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

But as for someone who comes hurrying to you,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

while he fears [Allah],
‘Abasa – He Frowned

you are neglectful of him.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

No indeed! These [verses of the Quran ] are a reminder
‘Abasa – He Frowned

—so let anyone who wishes remember—
‘Abasa – He Frowned

in honoured scriptures,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

exalted and purified,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

in the hands of envoys,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

noble and pious.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

Perish man! How ungrateful is he!
‘Abasa – He Frowned

From what did He create him?
‘Abasa – He Frowned

He created him from a drop of [seminal] fluid; then proportioned him.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

Then He made the way easy for him;
‘Abasa – He Frowned

then He made him die and buried him;
‘Abasa – He Frowned

and then, when He wished, resurrected him.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

No indeed! He has not yet carried out what He has commanded him.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

Let man consider his food:
‘Abasa – He Frowned

We pour down plenteous water [from the sky],
‘Abasa – He Frowned

then We split the earth making fissures in it
‘Abasa – He Frowned

and make the grain grow in it,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

as well as vines and vegetables,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

olives and date palms,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

and densely-planted gardens,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

fruits and pastures,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

as a sustenance for you and your livestock.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

So when the deafening Cry comes—
‘Abasa – He Frowned

the day when a man will evade his brother,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

his mother and his father,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

his spouse and his sons—
‘Abasa – He Frowned

arabic text: لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِّنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ
english translation text:
each of them will have a task to keep him preoccupied on that day.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

Some faces will be bright on that day,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

laughing and joyous,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

and some faces on that day will be covered with dust,
‘Abasa – He Frowned

overcast with gloom.
‘Abasa – He Frowned

It is they who are the faithless, the vicious.
‘Abasa – He Frowned
